L2().details: Sets up the L2 contracts. Depends on `L1()` being called first.

disableUpgradedFork().details: Disables upgrade mode for testing. By default the fork testing env will be upgraded to the latest      implementation. This can be used to disable the upgrade which, is useful for tests targeting the upgrade      process itself.

enableAltDA().details: Enables alternative data availability mode for testing

returnIfForkTest(string).details: Returns early when running against a forked production network. Useful for allowing a portion of a test      to run.

skipIfCoverage().details: Skips tests when running in coverage mode.

skipIfForkTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network.

skipIfOpsRepoTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network using the superchain ops repo.

params._amount: Amount of ETH (in wei) to call the burn function with.
Amount of ETH (in wei) to call the burn function with.
params._caller: Address of the caller to call the burn function with.

params._amount: Amount of ETH (in wei) to call the mint function with.
Amount of ETH (in wei) to call the mint function with.
params._caller: Address of the caller to call the mint function with.

STARTING_LIQUIDITY_BALANCE().notice: The starting balance of the ETHLiquidity contract.

artifacts().notice: The address of the Artifacts contract. Set into state by Deployer.setUp() with `etch` to avoid         mutating any nonces. MUST not have constructor logic.

isForkTest().notice: Indicates whether a test is running against a forked production network.

testFuzz_burn_fromAuthorizedCaller_succe.notice: Tests that the burn function can always be called by an authorized caller.

testFuzz_burn_fromUnauthorizedCaller_fai.notice: Tests that the burn function always reverts when called by an unauthorized caller.

testFuzz_mint_fromAuthorizedCaller_succe.notice: Tests that the mint function can always be called by an authorized caller.

testFuzz_mint_fromUnauthorizedCaller_fai.notice: Tests that the mint function always reverts when called by an unauthorized caller.

test_mint_moreThanAvailableBalance_fails.notice: Tests that the mint function fails when the amount requested is greater than the         available balance. In practice this should never happen because the starting         balance is expected to be uint248 wei, the total ETH supply is far less than that         amount, and the only contract that pulls from here is the SuperchainWETH contract         which will always burn ETH somewhere before minting it somewhere else. It needs to         be a system-wide invariant that this condition is never triggered in the first         place but it is the behavior we expect if it does happen.

test_setup_succeeds().notice: Tests that contract is set up with the correct starting balance.

STARTING_LIQUIDITY_BALANCE().notice: The starting balance of the ETHLiquidity contract.

In practice this should never happen because the starting         balance is expected to be uint248 wei, the total ETH supply is far less than that         amount, and the only contract that pulls from here is the SuperchainWETH contract         which will always burn ETH somewhere before minting it somewhere else. It needs to         be a system-wide invariant that this condition is never triggered in the first         place but it is the behavior we expect if it does happen.
